_comment: Status data for gen-coverage.py. Keys: 'command' or 'command.variant'. Only non-default entries needed.

accessibility_action.notes: Test surface added: adjustable counter, custom actions, magic tap

accessibility_action.decrement.notes: AdjustableCounterView with .adjustable trait; decrements counter from 5

accessibility_action.escape.notes: Escape via responder chain — works from any screen

accessibility_action.increment.notes: AdjustableCounterView with .adjustable trait; increments counter from 5

accessibility_action.invoke.notes: Invokes named custom actions on a11y_custom_actions element

accessibility_action.list.notes: Lists custom actions on a11y_custom_actions (Mark as Read, Archive) and adjustable trait on a11y_adjustable

accessibility_action.magic_tap.notes: VC overrides accessibilityPerformMagicTap(); status label updates to confirm

accessibility_events.notes: New command. Subscribes to UIAccessibility notifications (announcements, focus changes) and VC lifecycle events. Ring buffer of 500 events. Enables event-driven wait_for.

animations.scan.notes: BUG-009 fix (icon catalog background thread crash) was unrelated to this failure. 'Response lost in transit' issue persists — server logs success but client never receives response. trace and speed actions work fine. New issue: #267.

animations.speed.notes: Sets/gets CALayer animation speed. Returns {speed:N, animations_enabled:bool}. Tested: set 0.5 and 1.0 — both accepted. Note: returned speed always shows 1 regardless of value param (may be reading before set takes effect, similar to timeline.config async dispatch).

animations.trace.notes: Returns {point, samples:[...]} with position, bounds, opacity, window_position, t_ms for each sample. Sampled ~5 frames over ~90ms at (200,400). Captures layer animation state at a point over time.

appearance.notes: New command. Toggles light/dark mode via overrideUserInterfaceStyle on all visible windows.

assert.notes: New command. Assertion primitives for agent-driven testing. Supports element state, text presence, and count assertions.

back.notes: BUG-001 FIXED: BackHandler uses pepper_effectiveNavController to find child UINavigationController from SwiftUI NavigationStack. pepper_canPop checks nav bar items as secondary signal. pepper_popBack uses HID back-button tap for SwiftUI NavigationStack.

batch.notes: Executes array of commands sequentially, returns all responses. Tested: (1) ping+screen returns 2 responses with correct data. (2) tap+look returns tap result then screen state. (3) Empty array returns {executed:0, errors:0, responses:[]}. (4) Missing commands param returns proper error.
batch.surface: Sequence of ping + screen, tap + look

clipboard.clear.notes: Clears UIPasteboard. Returns {ok:true}. Verified: subsequent get returns count=0, empty types array.

clipboard.get.notes: Returns string, count, and types array. After set('Hello Pepper'), get returns {string:'Hello Pepper', count:1, types:['public.utf8-plain-text']}. After clear, returns {count:0, types:[]}.

clipboard.set.notes: Sets clipboard string via UIPasteboard. Returns {ok:true, value:'Hello Pepper'}. Verified by subsequent get.

concurrency.notes: Swift Concurrency runtime inspector. Lists active tasks, enumerates actor classes via metadata flags, reads task priority/state, supports task cancellation by address.

console.clear.notes: Returns {cleared:true}. Verified buffer_count and total_captured reset to 0 after clear.

console.log.notes: Returns {count:N, lines:[]}. Supports limit and filter params. Test app produces no stdout/stderr output so lines are always empty, but command structure and params work correctly.

console.status.notes: Returns active, buffer_size, buffer_count, total_captured. Tested before/after start/stop/clear. State transitions are correct.

console.stop.notes: Returns {active:false, total_captured:N}. Correctly deactivates capture. Verified status shows active=false after stop.

constraints.notes: LayoutTestViewController added with 4 sections: (1) well-constrained 3-label stack, (2) intentionally ambiguous view (missing trailing/width — flagged by ambiguous_only), (3) two conflicting required width constraints (100pt vs 200pt), (4) complex nested view tree with constraints at 3 depths. Navigate: Misc tab → UIKit → Layout Test Screen.

cookies.clear.notes: Clears all cookies. Returns {removed:0}. Command works, needs WKWebView for full verification with actual cookie data.

cookies.delete.notes: Deletes cookie by domain+name. Tested: domain=httpbin.org name=test returns {removed:0}. Command works, needs WKWebView for full verification.

cookies.get.notes: Filters cookies by domain. Tested: domain=httpbin.org returns {count:0, cookies:[]}. Command works, needs WKWebView for full verification.

cookies.list.notes: Returns cookies from HTTPCookieStorage. Works correctly — returns {count:0, total:0, cookies:[]} since no WKWebView/cookies exist in test app. Command functions properly but needs WKWebView surface for full data verification.

coredata.notes: Core Data schema inspector. Discovers NSPersistentContainer via AppDelegate/common singletons. Action: entities — lists entity names, attributes, and relationships from the managed object model.

crashes.notes: Captures uncaught ObjC exceptions and fatal signals (SIGABRT, SIGSEGV, SIGBUS, SIGTRAP) with symbolicated stacks. Persists to disk, loads on relaunch. Actions: status, list, clear.

db.notes: Blocked: db command not yet implemented (#417). Test surface ready: pepper_test.db seeded at app launch (AppSeeding.swift) with users(10), posts(10), settings(10) tables covering TEXT/INTEGER/REAL/BLOB column types. Core Data SQLite store at PepperCoreData.sqlite also seeded with Item entity (10 rows: title/count/value/createdAt). Both files live in Library/Application Support/.

deeplinks.notes: Returns {count:0, deeplinks:[], note:'No deep links configured...'} in generic mode. Command works correctly; no adapter configured so no deeplinks available.

defaults.delete.notes: Removes UserDefaults key. Returns {key, removed:true}. Verified: subsequent get returns 'Key not found' error.

defaults.get.notes: Returns single key value with type. Tested: get test_string returned {value:'hello world', type:'string'}. Missing key returns proper error: 'Key not found'.

defaults.list.notes: Returns paginated list of all UserDefaults entries. Found 790 total (200 per page). Each entry has key, type (string/bool/int/float/array/dict), and value. Includes system defaults (WebKit*, Apple*, PL*) and app-set values.

defaults.set.notes: Sets UserDefaults key. Tested: set test_string='hello world' (string), set test_number=42 (stored as string '42'). Returns {ok:true, key, value}. Verified via subsequent get.

dialog.auto_dismiss.notes: Enable with buttons=['OK','Cancel']: returns {auto_dismiss:true, buttons:['OK','Cancel'], delay:0.3}. Triggered alert via Show Alert — dialog auto-dismissed within 1s (current returned has_dialog=false). Disable: returns auto_dismiss=false. Default buttons include 'Allow While Using App', 'Allow Once', 'Allow', 'OK'.

dialog.current.notes: Returns most recent pending dialog with has_dialog=true and full dialog data. When no dialog present: returns has_dialog=false, dialog=null.

dialog.detect_system.notes: Returns {detected, confidence, positive_signals, total_signals, signals[]}. Four signals: intercepted_dialog, key_window_level, elevated_alert_windows, hit_test_probe. Confidence: high (3+), medium (2), low (1), none (0). On fresh install: tap request_notifications_button, PepperWindowMonitor auto-dismisses via elevated_window_button strategy; detect_system returns detected=false after dismissal. look works immediately after (no timeout).
dialog.detect_system.surface: Misc tab — request_notifications_button triggers system dialog on fresh install

dialog.dismiss.notes: Dismisses dialog by button name. Tested: dismiss with button='OK' returns {dismissed:true, button:'OK'}. Screen returns to normal 'ui' state. Error case: dismiss with no pending dialog returns proper error 'No pending dialog to dismiss'.

dialog.dismiss_sheet.notes: After triggering share sheet via share button, dismiss_sheet returns {dismissed:true} and sheet is removed. Interceptor broadcasts share_sheet_dismissed event.

dialog.list.notes: Returns array of pending dialogs with dialog_id, title, message, actions (title, style, index), timestamp. Tested: triggered alert shows 1 dialog with title='Test Alert', message='This is a test alert dialog', 2 actions (OK default, Cancel cancel). Empty list returns count=0.

dialog.share_sheet.notes: Share button presents UIActivityViewController via UIKit (bypasses SwiftUI .sheet). Interceptor detects sheet with has_sheet=true, items=['Shared from PepperTestApp'], and sheet_id.

diff.notes: Quick view hierarchy diff. Delegates to snapshot infrastructure with reserved baseline. Regression test: diff start/show/clear lifecycle on Snapshot Test Screen.

dismiss.notes: BUG-008 FIXED: DismissHandler now checks modalPresentationStyle (pageSheet/formSheet/automatic) instead of chain depth. SwiftUI .sheet() presentations correctly detected and dismissed.

dismiss_keyboard.notes: Returns {dismissed:true} in all cases. Tested: (1) no keyboard showing — returns dismissed:true (no-op). (2) After input command focuses text_field — keyboard active, dismiss_keyboard dismisses it. No error cases.

dynamic_type.notes: New command. Overrides UIContentSizeCategory via traitOverrides on all visible windows. Same runtime override pattern as appearance/locale.

explore.call.notes: Blocked: explore command not yet implemented (#405). Test surface ready: use IntrospectionWidget.incrementScore() to verify method invocation changes score property value.

explore.edit.notes: Blocked: explore command not yet implemented (#405). Test surface ready: IntrospectionWidget.caption (NSString) and score (NSNumber) are writable — verify value changes persist and reflect in heap.inspect.

explore.ivars.notes: Blocked: explore command not yet implemented (#405). Test surface ready: IntrospectionWidget and IntrospectionUser have private Swift ivars (_secret, _internalToken) that are NOT @objc — validates that private ivars are hidden from ObjC runtime enumeration.

explore.methods.notes: Blocked: explore command not yet implemented (#405). Test surface ready: IntrospectionWidget has activate/deactivate/reset/incrementScore. IntrospectionUser has activate/deactivate/resetScore. IntrospectionController has reset.

explore.overview.notes: Blocked: explore command not yet implemented (#405). Test surface ready: IntrospectionController (accessible via controller variable), deep chain controller.model.user.name, mix of CGRect/UIColor/UIFont/NSDate/BOOL/NSString/NSNumber properties.

explore.properties.notes: Blocked: explore command not yet implemented (#405). Test surface ready: IntrospectionWidget UIView subclass has widgetFrame (CGRect), tintOverride (UIColor), labelFont (UIFont), creationDate (NSDate), isWidgetEnabled (BOOL), caption (NSString), score (NSNumber).

find.count.notes: Returned count=27 for type=='button'. Also tested label CONTAINS 'Tap' (count=1). Correct counts for known elements.

find.first.notes: Returns first matching element with label, type, center, traits, tap_cmd, view_controller, total_matches=27. No-match predicate returns proper 'No elements match predicate' error.

find.list.notes: Returns array of matches with count and per-element details (label, center, type, traits, tap_cmd). Limit param works correctly, capping results at 5.

flags.notes: Feature flag overrides stored in UserDefaults. CRUD lifecycle: list → set → get → clear.

flags.clear.notes: Clears one or all overrides. Without key returns cleared count. Smoke test clears all after set.

flags.get.notes: Returns key, value, has_override. Smoke test sets then gets pepper_smoke_test_flag.

flags.list.notes: Returns count and overrides array. Smoke test verifies has_keys: count, overrides.

flags.set.notes: Stores override in UserDefaults. Returns key, value, ok. Smoke test sets boolean flag.

formatters.notes: New command. Returns active locale formatters (date, number, currency) and their current output.

frameworks.notes: Enumerates loaded dylibs/frameworks via Mach-O headers

frameworks.detail.notes: Detailed info for a single loaded image

gesture.pinch.notes: Executes pinch gesture at point. Returns {gesture:'pinch', start_distance:200, end_distance:50, center}. Scale parameter controls zoom. Command injects multi-touch HID events correctly.

gesture.rotate.notes: UIRotationGestureRecognizer on RotatableView. Rotates a hex shape and displays cumulative angle in degrees (rotation_angle label). gesture.rotate injects HID rotation events; angle label updates on each gesture change.

hangs.notes: Main thread hang detection with symbolicated stack capture via Mach thread APIs. Actions: start, stop, status, hangs, clear. Configurable threshold_ms (default 250).

heap.classes.notes: Pattern 'State' found 501 classes (showing 100). Pattern 'PepperTest' found 32 app-specific classes. Pattern 'Controller' with limit=10 correctly capped results. Missing pattern param returns proper error.

heap.controllers.notes: Returns 11 controllers: root UIHostingController, UIKitTabBarController (3 tabs, selected=0), 3 TabHostingControllers, 3 UIKitNavigationControllers (stack_count=1 each), 3 NavigationStackHostingControllers. Shows depth, isVisible, title, and tab/nav metadata.
heap.controllers.surface: Full VC hierarchy from key window

heap.inspect.notes: Returns Mirror-based property dump. Found UITabBarController at address via vc_hierarchy strategy. Shows 5 properties (popoverBridge, update, etc.). AppState not findable (no singleton/VC/view match). Missing class param returns proper error.

heap.read.notes: Reads ObjC properties via KVC. Tested: selectedIndex=0 (type __NSCFNumber), viewControllers returns 3 TabHostingControllers. Invalid key_path returns proper KVC error. Missing key_path param returns proper error.

heap_snapshot.check.notes: Automatic leak detection with severity classification (high/medium/low). Takes baseline first, then check compares. Smoke test verifies leak_count and verdict keys.

heap_snapshot.clear.notes: Clears saved snapshot. Returns {cleared:true}. Confirmed status returns has_snapshot=false after clear, and diff returns proper 'No baseline snapshot' error.

heap_snapshot.diff.notes: Compares current heap to baseline. After ~10s found 7 growing classes (CString +10, Endpoint +8, etc.) with verdict '7 class(es) growing — potential leaks'. Returns growing/shrinking arrays with before/after/delta. No-baseline error handled properly.

heap_snapshot.snapshot.notes: Scans heap via malloc zone enumeration. Returns total_classes=115, total_instances, top_30 by count, memory info (resident_mb, virtual_mb). Top classes: AccessibilityGeometryStorage (342), AccessibilityNode (185), _LocaleICU (152).

heap_snapshot.status.notes: Without snapshot: returns {has_snapshot:false}. After snapshot: returns has_snapshot=true, class_count=115, taken_at ISO8601 timestamp, seconds_ago.

help.notes: Returns list of 50 available server commands. Tested via pepper-ctl and direct WebSocket.

highlight.notes: Highlighted 'Tap Me' button. Returns frame, description, strategy (interactive_text), highlighted=true.
highlight.surface: Tap Me button on Controls tab

hook.clear.notes: Clears log for specific hook. Returns {cleared:'hook_1'}. Subsequent log returns count=0, empty entries.

hook.install.notes: Installed hook on PepperTestApp.UIKitControlsViewController viewDidAppear:. Returns {hook_id:'hook_1', class, method, class_method:false}. System classes (UIViewController) are properly rejected with safety error. Missing class/method params return proper errors.

hook.list.notes: Returns array of hooks with id, class, method, class_method, installed_at, call_count. Empty list returns count=0. After install shows 1 hook with call_count=0.

hook.log.notes: Tab switch (Controls→List→Controls) triggered viewDidAppear: hook. Log returned 6 entries with receiver, receiver_class, method, timestamp, args (['YES']), call_number. Nonexistent hook ID returns empty entries (count=0).
hook.log.surface: After install + trigger via tab switch

hook.remove.notes: Removes hook by ID. Returns {removed:'hook_1'}. Subsequent list confirms hook removed (count=0). Nonexistent hook ID returns proper 'Hook not found' error.

hook.remove_all.notes: Installed 2 hooks (viewDidAppear: and viewWillAppear:), then remove_all. Returns {removed:'all'}. Subsequent list confirms count=0.

identify_icons.notes: Command works correctly. Returns catalog info (built=true) but icon_count=0 because test app uses SF Symbols (system icons), not custom bundled icon assets. icon_name matching requires app-bundled assets.

identify_selected.notes: Correctly identified 'Size' as selected. Returns scores, debug info with brightness/ink analysis, and detected color scheme (light).

input.notes: TextField: set 'Hello Pepper', appended ' World' with --no-clear, cleared+replaced with 'Replaced'. TextEditor (text_view): set value accepted. Error case: nonexistent ID returns proper 'Element not found' error.

introspect.accessibility.notes: Returns 62 accessibility elements with label, type, traits, frame, interactive flag, view_controller, scroll_context, presentation_context. Covers buttons, headers, staticText, segments, sliders.

introspect.full.notes: Returns combined accessibility (250 elements) + viewHierarchy (126 views) + hostingControllerCount (7). Default mode when no mode param given. Response ~52-96KB depending on tab. Tested on Controls and List tabs.

introspect.interactive.notes: Returns 38 elements (28 labeled + 10 unlabeled) with hit-test filtering, gesture info, source tracking (accessibility vs view), center coordinates, is_control flag, label_source. Most detailed interactive mode.

introspect.map.notes: Returns spatially-grouped screen state (rows by y-range). 11 rows, 15 elements on List tab. Includes memory_mb, screen, element_count, non_interactive text. This is the backend for 'look' command. Error case: invalid mode returns proper error message.

introspect.mirror.notes: Returns Mirror-based SwiftUI type reflection. Found 2 mirrors: _UIHostingView with RootViewDelegate tree (depth 3) and UIKitPlatformViewHost (depth 4). Shows SwiftUI view structure.

introspect.platform.notes: Returns platform UIView hierarchy analysis. Shows UISwitch, UISegmentedControl, UISlider with frames, interactive flag, hasGestures, subviewCount, id, value. Good for UIKit introspection.

introspect.tappable.notes: Returns 29 tappable/interactive elements filtered from accessibility tree. Each has label, type, traits, frame, view_controller. Subset of accessibility mode.

introspect.text.notes: Returns all visible text on screen with frame, type (header, button, staticText). Found Buttons, Tap Me, Count: 3, Toggles, Segmented Control, etc.

keychain.clear.notes: Removes all keychain items. Returns {ok:true, cleared:true}. Verified: subsequent list returns count=0, empty items array.

keychain.delete.notes: Removes specific keychain item by service+account. Returns {ok:true, removed:true, service}. Verified: subsequent list shows item removed.

keychain.get.notes: Returns keychain item value. Tested: get service=com.pepper.test account=testuser returned {value:'s3cret123', type:'string', size:9}.

keychain.list.notes: Returns array of keychain items with service, account, access_group, created, modified timestamps. Tested: after set, shows 1 item. After clear, shows 0 items.

keychain.set.notes: Adds keychain item. Params: service, account, value. Returns {ok:true, action:'added', service, account}. Missing 'value' param returns proper error.

layers.notes: BUG-002 FIXED: no longer crashes on gradient views. Returns full CALayer tree with properties (cornerRadius, shadow, gradient colors/locations, opacity, masks). Tested: Misc tab gradient area returned GradientLayer with colors [#0088FF, #9069EF, #CB30E0], shadow properties. List tab returned CellHostingView layer tree. Controls tab times out (deep hierarchy) — not a crash, just slow. Error case: missing point param returns proper error.

lifecycle.background.notes: Returns {state:'background'}. Posts willResignActive + didEnterBackground notifications. App remains responsive (WebSocket stays connected). Error cases: missing action returns proper error, unknown action returns proper error.

lifecycle.cycle.notes: Returns {state:'backgrounding', foreground_delay:1}. Tested with delay=1.0. Backgrounds immediately, schedules foreground return after delay. App responsive after cycle completes.

lifecycle.foreground.notes: Returns {state:'active'}. Posts willEnterForeground + didBecomeActive notifications. Tested after background — app fully responsive, look returns normal screen state.

lifecycle.memory_warning.notes: Returns {triggered:true}. Posts didReceiveMemoryWarningNotification and calls _performMemoryWarning on UIApplication. App remains stable after trigger.

loading.surface: Any screen with spinners, progress bars, or skeleton views

locale.current.notes: Returns language ('en'), region ('US'), identifier ('en_US'), app_localizations (['en']), override_active (false). After set: adds override_language field showing active override.

locale.languages.notes: Returns {count:1, languages:['en']}. Shows available app localizations from bundle.

locale.lookup.notes: Command works correctly: returns {key, value, found}. Returns found=false for keys 'greeting' and 'button_tap' — Localizable.strings file exists in source but isn't properly included in app bundle (build config issue, not a Pepper bug). Missing key param returns proper error.

locale.reset.notes: Returns {override_active:false, restored:true}. Verified via current: override_active=false, language back to 'en', no override_language field.

locale.set.notes: Set language='es'. Returns {override_active:true, language:'es', region:'', note:'Locale override active...'}. Verified via current: shows override_language='es'. Note: affects NSLocalizedString resolution, existing views may need refresh.

memory.snapshot.notes: Returns {timestamp_ms, virtual_mb, footprint_mb, resident_mb}. Tested: resident_mb=586.3, footprint_mb=205.07. Provides process-level memory snapshot via task_info.

memory.vm.notes: Returns {timestamp_ms, phys_footprint_mb, internal_mb, compressed_mb, purgeable_mb}. Tested: phys_footprint_mb=205.1, internal_mb=157, compressed_mb=44.52. More granular VM region breakdown.

navigate.deeplink.notes: URL scheme auto-detected from Info.plist. peppertest://controls, peppertest://list, peppertest://misc switch tabs via onOpenURL. Other routes show generic DeeplinkView modal.

navigate.dismiss.notes: BUG-008 FIXED: NavigateHandler handlePop falls through to modal dismiss when nav stack is at root depth=1 (SwiftUI .sheet() case). SwiftUI .sheet() presentations now correctly dismissed.

navigate.pop.notes: BUG-001 FIXED: pepper_effectiveNavController now walks child VCs to find SwiftUI NavigationStack's UINavigationController. pepper_effectiveDepth uses nav bar item count as secondary signal. pepper_popBack falls back to HID back-button tap for SwiftUI-managed nav.
navigate.pop.surface: Detail nav stack (pushed via Item 0 tap)

network.clear.notes: Returns {cleared:true}. Verified buffer_count drops to 0 after clear, total_recorded preserved.

network.clear_conditions.notes: Regression test: add 2 conditions → clear_conditions → verify conditions count drops to 0. Part of conditions lifecycle test.

network.clear_mocks.notes: Regression test: add 2 mocks → clear_mocks → verify mocks count drops to 0. Dedicated clear_mocks regression test.

network.conditions.notes: Regression test: simulate latency with known id → call conditions → verify entry appears. Part of conditions lifecycle test.

network.log.notes: Returns {count:N, transactions:[...]}. Each transaction has request (method, url, headers), response (status_code, headers, body, content_length), timing, and id. Captured GET https://httpbin.org/json → 200 with full JSON body. Limit param works correctly.

network.mock.notes: Regression test: mock pepper.test/mock with status 200 + JSON body → tap Mock Test → verify network_body_preview shows mocked content. Auto-starts interception.
network.mock.surface: Mock Test button (MiscTab Network → mock_request_button → https://pepper.test/mock)

network.mocks.notes: Regression test: add mock → call mocks → verify entry appears with url pattern and status code. Part of mock lifecycle test.

network.presets.notes: Regression test: call presets → verify returns named presets (3G, Edge, LTE, WiFi, High Latency DNS, 100% Loss) with effect descriptions.

network.remove_condition.notes: Regression test: simulate with known id → remove_condition by id → verify conditions list empty. Part of conditions lifecycle test.

network.remove_mock.notes: Regression test: add mock → remove_mock by id → verify mocks list empty. Part of mock lifecycle test.

network.simulate.notes: Parent action for effect-based conditions (latency, fail_status, fail_error, throttle, offline) and preset-based simulation. Auto-starts interception. All 5 effects have regression tests.

network.simulate.fail_error.notes: Regression test: simulate fail_error NSURLErrorDomain/-1009 → tap Error Request → verify network_error shows domain/code. Cleans up via clear_conditions.

network.simulate.fail_status.notes: Regression test: simulate fail_status 500 → tap Error Request → verify network_status_code shows HTTP 500. Cleans up via clear_conditions.

network.simulate.latency.notes: Regression test: simulate latency 2000ms → tap Timed Request → verify elapsed >= 2000ms. Auto-starts interception. Cleans up via clear_conditions.

network.simulate.offline.notes: Regression test: simulate offline → tap Offline Test → verify network_error shows offline NSError. Cleans up via clear_conditions.

network.simulate.throttle.notes: Regression test: simulate throttle 256 bytes/s → tap Timed Request → verify elapsed significantly > unthrottled baseline. Cleans up via clear_conditions.

network.start.notes: Returns {active:true, buffer_size:500}. Accepts buffer_size param. URLProtocol-based interception activates correctly.

network.status.notes: Returns active, buffer_size, buffer_count, total_recorded. All fields accurate across start/stop/clear lifecycle.

network.ws_clear.notes: Regression test: connect WebSocket → ws_clear → verify ws_log returns 0 frames. Part of WebSocket lifecycle test.

network.ws_log.notes: Regression test: connect WebSocket → ws_log → verify frames returned. Supports limit, filter, direction params. Part of WebSocket lifecycle test.

network.ws_status.notes: Regression test: connect WebSocket → ws_status → verify active connections >= 1. Returns active, buffer_size, frame_count, total_recorded, active_connections.

notifications.notes: Surface added (TASK-316). NSNotificationCenter inspector — register_observer_button, post_notification_button, remove_observer_button, and notification_received_count label all available.

notifications.clear.notes: Clears all tracked observers and events. Returns cleared=true.

notifications.counts.notes: Surface added (TASK-316). After registering observer, counts shows PepperTestNotification observer entry.

notifications.events.notes: Surface added (TASK-316). Events logged after post. Shows add/remove/post timeline.

notifications.list.notes: Lists observers with filter/limit support. Returns count and observers array.

notifications.post.notes: Surface added (TASK-316). post_notification_button triggers PepperTestNotification. notification_received_count label increments on receive. Also testable via notifications post name=PepperTestNotification.

notifications.start.notes: Surface added (TASK-316). register_observer_button registers an observer for PepperTestNotification. notifications start then list/counts shows the observer.

notifications.status.notes: Returns active, observer_count, event_count, total_tracked. Smoke test verifies active and observer_count keys.

notifications.stop.notes: Stops tracking. Returns active=false. Smoke test verifies has_keys: active.

orientation.landscape_left.notes: Command accepted, UIDevice.setValue + orientationDidChangeNotification posted. Response still reports 'portrait' because UIWindowScene.interfaceOrientation doesn't change from programmatic UIDevice.setValue in simulator. Not a bug — simulator has no accelerometer. Error case: invalid value returns proper error.

orientation.landscape_right.notes: Same behavior as landscape_left — command succeeds, notification posted, but UIWindowScene reads portrait in simulator.

orientation.portrait.notes: Returns {orientation:'portrait', is_portrait:true, is_landscape:false}. Command accepted, notification posted. Query-only (no value param) also works.

orientation.portrait_upside_down.notes: Same behavior — command succeeds, notification posted. UIWindowScene still reads portrait in simulator.

perf.notes: New command. Frame performance profiler using CADisplayLink — measures frame timing, dropped frames, hitch rate, and FPS.

push.notes: Delivers local push notification. Tested: title='Test Push', body='Hello from Pepper', delay=0. Returns {delivered:true, notification_id:'pepper-push-ADF38CB4', title, body}. No UNNotification delegate needed — uses UNUserNotificationCenter directly.

read.notes: Works for UIKit elements: read sort_picker returned value=2, segmentCount=3, segmentTitles, enabled, visible. Fails for SwiftUI elements (BUG-006: tap_button returns 'Element not found'). Nonexistent IDs return proper 'Element not found' error.

renders.notes: New command. SwiftUI view tree snapshots via makeViewDebugData() and snapshot diffing.

responder_chain.notes: Dumps gesture recognizers, responder chain, and hit-test path. Smoke test hits point (200,400). Regression test uses text='Tap Me'.
responder_chain.surface: Any interactive element (Controls tab button for regression)

sandbox.notes: App sandbox file system browser. List, read, write, delete files in Documents/Library/Caches/tmp. Supports plist/JSON auto-detection and directory size summaries. Seeded: documents/notes.json, documents/settings.plist, caches/cached-image.txt, tmp/temp-data.txt.

scroll.bottom.notes: Scrolled to bottom; Item 29 visible at bottom

scroll.left.notes: Horizontal ScrollView already present; scroll left shifts content leftward

scroll.right.notes: Horizontal ScrollView already present; scroll right shifts content rightward

scroll.top.notes: Scrolled to top from mid-list; Item 0 visible at top

scroll.up.notes: Scrolled up 400pt from bottom; items shifted upward as expected

scroll_to.down.notes: Scrolls down until exact text match found. Tested: Item 12 found after 1 scroll (1325ms), Item 29 found after 1 scroll. Already-visible returns immediately (14ms). BUG-004: server dispatch timeout (10s) fires before long scrolls complete.
scroll_to.down.surface: List tab — scroll until text visible

scroll_to.left.notes: Horizontal ScrollView available for left-direction scroll_to validation

scroll_to.right.notes: Horizontal ScrollView available for right-direction scroll_to validation

scroll_to.up.notes: Scrolls up to find text. Tested: Item 5 found after 2 scrolls (2648ms) from bottom of list. BUG-004 applies for long distances.

snapshot.clear.notes: Deletes all saved snapshots. Regression test: save two snapshots, clear, verify list empty.

snapshot.delete.notes: Deletes a saved snapshot by name. Regression test: save, delete, verify removed from list.

snapshot.diff.notes: Compares current screen state against saved snapshot. Returns added/removed/changed elements and text. Supports ignore_transient and assert_no_diff. Regression tests: no-change diff, post-mutation diff, assert_no_diff, ignore_transient.

snapshot.list.notes: Lists all saved snapshot names. Regression test: verified in lifecycle and clear tests.

snapshot.save.notes: Captures current look/introspect map output as a named baseline for later diffing. Regression test: save and verify element_count > 0.

status.notes: Returns port, connections count, and connectionDetails array (id, connectedAt, lastActivity, subscriptions). Tested via direct WebSocket.

subscribe.notes: Subscribes to event types via events array. Tested: subscribe events=['screen_change'] returns {subscribed:['screen_change']}. Missing 'events' param returns proper error.

swiftui_body.notes: Per-view SwiftUI body tracking via Mach-O protocol conformance scanning and hosting view hooking.

swipe.down.notes: Swiped down from center (201,437) to (201,837). List scrolled back to top showing Item 0. Swipe down = finger moves down = content scrolls down.

swipe.left.notes: Swiped left from center (201,437) to (-199,437). Command executed without error. No swipe-action rows in test app to reveal delete/actions, but gesture injected correctly.

swipe.right.notes: Swiped right from center (201,437) to (601,437). Command executed without error. Gesture injected correctly.

swipe.up.notes: Swiped up from center (201,437) to (201,37). List scrolled from Items 0-9 to Items 11-24. Swipe up = finger moves up = content scrolls up.

swizzle_check.notes: New command. Calls each swizzled authorization API and verifies the swizzle intercepted (completion fired synchronously with auto-grant value). Covers: notifications, photos, tracking, camera, contacts, location, eventkit_events, eventkit_reminders.

tap.element.notes: BUG-006 FIXED: PepperElementResolver now falls back to PepperSwiftUIBridge.collectAccessibilityElements() when UIView hierarchy lookup fails. SwiftUI .accessibilityIdentifier() elements (e.g. tap_button) are now discoverable via the accessibility tree.

tap.heuristic.notes: Tapped slider via heuristic:'slider'. Found at (116,790). Error case also tested: nonexistent heuristic returns proper error.

tap.icon_name.notes: Custom icon assets added to test app asset catalog. Icon-only buttons displayed on Controls tab. Ready for testing with tap icon_name=pepper-home.
tap.icon_name.surface: Custom icon asset buttons (pepper-home, pepper-camera, pepper-search)

tap.point.notes: Tapped Tap Me button by coordinate. Count incremented from 0 to 1. Strategy: point.

tap.predicate.notes: Predicate "label == 'Tap Me' AND type == 'button'" found and tapped correctly. Count incremented. Error case tested: no-match predicate returns proper error.

tap.tab.notes: BUG-005 FIXED: Added findAccessibilityTabButtons() that scans the accessibility tree for the .tabBar container and its .button children. Works for SwiftUI TabView (tabs found via accessibility elements). Programmatic UITabBarController selection also available as further fallback.

tap.text.surface: Tap Me button, tab bar items

target_actions.notes: Lists UIControl target-action pairs. Walks view tree, enumerates allTargets and actions(forTarget:forControlEvent:).
target_actions.surface: Any screen with UIControl subclasses (buttons, switches, sliders)

test.reset.notes: Resets test state. Returns {reset:true}. Works both with and without active test session.

test.result.notes: Records test result. Tested: result with test_id='task024', status='pass', notes='All good'. Returns {test_id, timestamp, status:'pass'}.

test.start.notes: Starts test session with test_id. Returns {test_id, status:'started'}. Missing test_id returns proper error.

timeline.clear.notes: Returns {cleared:true}. Verified buffer_count drops to 0 after clear. total_recorded preserved (historical count). Error case: unknown action returns proper error listing available actions.

timeline.config.notes: Accepts buffer_size, recording (bool), enabled_types params. Tested: buffer_size 2000→5000 (verified via subsequent status). recording=false pauses recording. Note: config response returns pre-update values due to async barrier dispatch — subsequent status shows correct values.

timeline.query.notes: Returns {count:N, events:[...]}. Each event has type, summary, timestamp_ms. Supports limit param, types array filter (tested ['network'] correctly filters to network-only), and filter text search. Showed command and network events with summaries like '200 GET /json (51ms, 429B)'.

timeline.status.notes: Returns recording, buffer_size, buffer_count, total_recorded, enabled_types. Always-on flight recorder starts automatically. Default enabled_types: network, screen, command, console.

timers.invalidate.notes: Surface added (TASK-316). timers invalidate can stop the NSTimer created via start_repeating_timer_button; stop_repeating_timer_button and stop_display_link_button also available for manual control.

timers.list.notes: Surface added (TASK-316). Active timers visible after tapping Start Timer/Start Display Link. repeating_timer_count and display_link_frame_count labels confirm activity.

timers.start.notes: Surface added (TASK-316). Use start_repeating_timer_button and start_display_link_button to create active NSTimer and CADisplayLink instances for timers tracking.

timers.status.notes: Surface added (TASK-316). Shows timer_count and display_link_count after starting timers.

timers.stop.notes: Surface added (TASK-316). Uninstalls tracker; combine with stop buttons in test app.

toggle.notes: BUG-006 fix applied to PepperElementResolver (used by TapHandler) but ToggleHandler still calls pepper_findElement(id:) directly, bypassing the SwiftUI accessibility fallback. SwiftUI Toggle elements still return 'Element not found'. Segmented control (sort_picker) works. New issue: #265.

tree.notes: Returns full UIView hierarchy as JSON (3355 lines on Controls tab). Supports --depth param for truncation (depth=3 returns 4 nodes). Reports nodeCount and truncated flag.

undo.notes: New command. NSUndoManager inspector — list undo managers, query undo/redo stack state, trigger undo/redo programmatically.

unsubscribe.notes: Unsubscribes from event types. Tested: unsubscribe events=['screen_change'] returns {unsubscribed:['screen_change']}. Missing 'events' param returns proper error.

unwatch.notes: Stops watch by ID or all. Tested: unwatch all=true returns {stopped_count:1, stopped:['w2']}. Nonexistent watch_id returns proper error.

vars.discover.notes: Force re-scans VC hierarchy for @Observable instances. Returns 2 instances (AppState with 17 properties, NestedState with 2 properties). Reports type, writable, value for each.

vars.dump.notes: Returns flat key-value dict of all properties for a class. Tested: dump AppState returns all 17 properties. Error case: nonexistent class returns proper error.

vars.get.notes: Returns single property value by path (ClassName.propertyName). Tested: AppState.tapCount=3, NestedState.innerValue='nested-hello'. Error cases: nonexistent property returns proper error, missing path param returns proper error.

vars.list.notes: BUG-003 FIXED: now returns 2 instances (AppState + NestedState) with all properties, types, writable flags. Previously returned 0 instances for @Observable classes.

vars.mirror.notes: Returns detailed Mirror-based property list with type, writable, published flags. Tested: mirror NestedState shows innerValue, innerCount, plus $observationRegistrar. More detail than list/dump.

vars.set.notes: Sets property value by path. Tested: set tapCount to 99 (confirmed via get), set NestedState.innerCount to 100. Returns {ok:true, value:newVal}. NOTE: does not trigger @Observable re-rendering — UI shows stale value until next natural update. Read-only properties return proper error.

verify.notes: New command. Explicit pass/fail assertions on screen state. Supports text presence, element state (visible/enabled/value), and screen name checks. Batch mode for multiple assertions in one call.

wait_for.exists.notes: BUG-007 FIXED: Background thread polling allows SwiftUI state changes. Text-based exists condition now detects async changes. Note: element ID condition in WaitHandler still uses pepper_findElement directly (UIKit-only); see #266 for full fix.

wait_for.has_value.notes: BUG-006 fix not applied to WaitHandler — WaitHandler.evaluate(.elementHasValue) calls pepper_findElement(id:) directly, bypassing SwiftUI accessibility fallback. SwiftUI counter elements still not found. New issue: #266.

wait_for.visible.notes: BUG-007 FIXED: Polling moved to background thread (DispatchQueue.global). Main thread is free for SwiftUI @Observable re-rendering. 3s timer test now works — wait_for detects async text change to 'Timer: FIRED'.
wait_for.visible.surface: Start 3s Timer → wait for FIRED text

wait_idle.notes: Returns {idle:bool, elapsed_ms:N}. Tested: returns idle=false after ~3s (animations/activity keep app non-idle). Accepts timeout param. Command functions correctly — monitors RunLoop/CADisplayLink activity.

watch.notes: Returns {watch_id, initial:{center, type, label, frame}}. Tested: watch label='Count' found 'Count: 0' staticText. Point-based watch also works: returns initial={} for empty area. Missing target returns proper error.
watch.surface: Watch counter label by label match

webview.notes: New command. Finds WKWebView instances, evaluates JavaScript, queries DOM. Actions: url (list webviews+URLs), evaluate (run JS), dom (DOM element listing via querySelectorAll).
